title: Storage, Filesystems, Archives, and Backups · Operations Recall
summary: Respond to low disk space and protect service data without confusing filesystems, mounts, archives, snapshots, and verified backups. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a storage map and restore-tested backup plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.
Distinguish apparent size, allocated blocks, free space, inodes, deleted-open files, and per-directory usage.
Use lsblk, findmnt, mount, and filesystem identifiers to observe topology before any mount or format action.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Block Filesystem Mount · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Filesystem / is 92% full with 500 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data. Competency probe: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.

Which action demonstrates Block Filesystem Mount without dropping earlier contracts?
